verdict.answer_first: A states the answer immediately: "TTL-based cache-aside with Redis"; B starts with "decision path" and "general shape".
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to one default: "default I'd run with"; B surveys many options: "In-process", "Redis / Memcached", "TTL only", "Write-through".
verdict.fabrication: B uses unsupported precise thresholds: "80% of read load is 10–20 distinct queries" and "hit rate below ~70–80%"; A has a softer unsupported claim: "large majority".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives a concrete first move: "Set TTLs of 30–60s... add TTL jitter + a per-key lock... ship one query behind a flag first."
